note: Offline-scored multi-domain SA corpus. Encodes the TEST 10-15 regressions as permanent cases + clean controls. Each case runs the full offline pipeline; scored on structured labels AND must_not_contain anti-contamination assertions. behavioral_guard is applied to EVERY case automatically.

cases.title: TEST 11 - offshore produced-water reinjection, vibration fatigue
cases.transcript: Offshore production platform produced water reinjection system, 24-inch A106 Gr B carbon steel. Design pressure 2,850 psi operating pressure 2,300 psi. Service produced water chlorides CO2 trace H2S. UT current wall 0.425 nominal 0.500 external corrosion survey. Significant vibration during high-rate injection, vibration increased over previous 8 months. Clamp support replaced 14 months ago. No vibration study conducted. Reinjection rates increased 40% last year. Pipe support 18 inches from a branch connection. Support shoe coating damage and metal-to-metal wear. Four years ago a nearly identical system had a branch connection fatigue crack. Tropical storm expected within 72 hours. 47 open maintenance items, 9 overdue, 3 vibration-related.

cases.title: TEST 13 - REAC inlet piping, NH4HS erosion-corrosion
cases.transcript: Refinery hydrocracker reactor effluent air cooler REAC inlet piping, 10-inch A106 Gr B carbon steel. Design pressure 2,150 psi operating pressure 1,875 psi. Service reactor effluent high ammonium bisulfide NH4HS sour H2S ammonia. Elbow nominal 0.365 in current wall 0.131 in. Localized metal loss at flow turns erosion-corrosion. Velocity increased after revamp raised charge rate 22 percent. Prior turnaround flagged thinning at same elbows no action taken. PT at elbow weld no indications no cracking found.
cases.baseline_note: REAC inlet piping misclassified as a vessel (->API 510) because 'reactor effluent air cooler' outscores 'piping' in the keyword tally - the cascade in miniature. EXIT GATE: must go GREEN when Phase 5 (Component->Asset->Authority derivation) + Phase 7 rule 3 (facility/equipment classification cannot override component classification) land, with NO REAC-specific keyword anywhere in the diff. If it needs a REAC keyword to pass, the derivation did not work and it has been whack-a-mole'd - that is a FAIL of the fix.

cases.title: TEST 14 - offshore gas compression/export, sour gas
cases.transcript: Offshore fixed production platform gas compression and export system, 36-inch A106 Gr B carbon steel discharge piping. Design pressure 1,850 psi operating pressure 1,420 psi. Service wet gas CO2 trace H2S produced water carryover. UT current wall 0.421 nominal 0.500. Compressor throughput increased 35 percent during the last 18 months. Increasing vibration during slugging. No vibration study performed since the throughput increase. No MOC documentation found. Pipe support 24 inches from a large branch connection, support shoe coating damage and fretting wear. Another facility had a branch connection fatigue crack and gas release. Major storm forecast within 96 hours. 61 open work orders, 14 overdue.

cases.title: TEST 15 - FLNG cryogenic LNG loading line
cases.transcript: Floating LNG FLNG facility cryogenic LNG export loading system, 24-inch stainless steel loading line connected to articulated marine loading arm. Design pressure 1,480 psi operating pressure 1,050 psi operating temperature -260F. Service LNG cryogenic methane thermal cycling marine environment. UT current wall 0.611 nominal 0.625 corrosion negligible. LNG transfer rates increased 28 percent. ESD activations increased from 1 per year to 8 per year. Increasing loading-arm movement alarms. Marine loading arm swivel replaced 18 months ago, no post-installation dynamic study performed. Pipe guide 12 inches from major expansion loop, guide shows abnormal wear, polished contact surfaces, deformation of wear pads, condition accepted as cosmetic. No motion analysis performed since loading-rate increase. Control software updated 7 months ago, no Management of Change package located.
cases.classification_review: LABEL REVIEW (not junk, not engine debt): FLNG resolves to offshore_platform, which is defensible (a floating LNG unit IS a classed offshore installation, marine/structural side) but misses the LNG-process side. Authority assertion currently passes. Resolve as a completeness/label question alongside T16, NOT lumped with the T19 genuine-misclassification smell.

cases.title: Control - routine fully-documented pressure vessel (no hidden risk)
cases.transcript: Pressure vessel shell, API 510 inspection current, thickness well above Tmin, low stable corrosion rate, all records complete, normal service, no severe weather, no operational change.

cases.title: Control - benign offshore seawater structural (no hydrocarbon)
cases.transcript: Offshore platform seawater lift caisson structural support, no hydrocarbon, general coating wear, thickness acceptable, no operational change, records current.

cases.title: TEST 16 - subsea tie-in: dynamic fatigue must lead the H2S->HIC screening
cases.transcript: Subsea pipeline tie-in assembly, 18-inch duplex stainless flowline connected to aging carbon steel manifold spool, offshore oil and gas, water depth 420 ft. Multiphase production oil gas produced water CO2 variable H2S sand production. UT localized external corrosion, flowline 0.750 to 0.734, spool 0.625 to 0.588, corrosion rate 0.0025 per year, 15+ year remaining life. PAUT no reportable indications, no cracking. Production increased 35 percent during previous 18 months. Intermittent slugging events increasing. Transient pressure excursions exceeded normal operating pressure by 20 percent, no engineering review completed. One support span partially lost seabed support, approximately 4 feet of unsupported span. Two CP anodes depleted. Progressive seabed scour. Similar field subsea flowline rupture from unsupported span vibration, slug flow fatigue, and inhibitor failures. Storm season beginning. Production optimization software installed 8 months ago, no Management of Change package located.
cases.fixture_correction: Subsea pipeline tie-in is governed by ASME B31.8 (gas)/B31.4 (liquid) + API 1111/DNV-ST-F101, NOT API 570 (in-service process piping). Expectation corrected; the derived B31.8 was the correct call. Engine NOT changed.

cases.title: TEST 17 - integrated hub system drift, NO physical damage mechanism (corrosion is a non-finding)
cases.transcript: Deepwater semi-submersible production hub 19 years, 14 subsea wells, oil export gas lift produced-water reinjection subsea boosting. No alarms no leaks no trips production online. 11 unrelated anomalies across different systems dispositioned independently, no common cause investigation. Produced water reinjection header 36-inch carbon steel UT remaining wall above minimum, corrosion rate stable, acceptable. Gas lift riser minor coating damage, no metal loss, no exposed steel. Subsea flowline pressure excursions increasing within design limits. Subsea booster pump vibration increased 18 percent below alarm. Gas compression three surge events. Mooring line utilization increased 52 to 81 percent within allowable. DP collision avoidance events increased 2 to 17. Control room staffing reduced 14 to 9 operators, overtime 72 hours per month. 241 open work orders, 67 overdue. Five transmitters intermittent signal loss. Production increased 27 percent over 24 months, no facility upgrades. 41 MOCs, 17 closed administratively, no field verification. Control software upgraded, vendor tuning modifications, no post-modification interaction study. Storm intensity increasing, higher wave loading, increased vessel and riser motion. ML anomaly detection cross-domain correlation confidence increasing.

cases.title: TEST 18 - refinery hydrogen network: control-instability drift, no material mechanism
cases.transcript: Integrated refinery hydrogen network, hydrocracker hydrotreater reformer hydrogen plant sulfur recovery, 22 years. No leaks no injuries no trips no code violations production at record levels. Corporate reliability dashboard hydrogen system instability index increasing for 7 consecutive months, no single asset flagged. Hydrocracker feed effluent circuit UT acceptable corrosion rate stable no reportable indications. Recycle gas compressor vibration plus 12 percent below alarm. All circuits above minimum thickness no active leaks. Refinery throughput increased 31 percent over 3 years, hydrogen consumption increased 27 percent, hydrogen purity decreased 98.7 to 95.4 percent still within limits. Advanced process control APC upgraded 14 months ago, hydrogen network optimization software installed 11 months ago, system hunts more frequently control loops continuously adjusting no alarm. Anti-surge activations 39 current year versus 11 previous year. Hydrogen analyzers intermittent disagreements analyzer A 95.4 B 97.1 C 96.2 no investigation. 312 open maintenance items 89 overdue. Senior hydrogen specialist retired position eliminated, hydrogen responsibilities distributed no single owner. Maximum utilization strategy, bonuses tied to throughput, no KPI for hydrogen network stability. Cross-unit hydrogen behavior correlation increasing confidence 91 percent. Every department reports equipment healthy, no group owns the problem.

cases.title: TEST 19 - 500kV grid: emergent network instability, no failed asset (cross-domain SYSTEM_DRIFT generalization)
cases.transcript: National electrical grid 500 kV transmission corridor connecting nuclear gas solar wind battery storage. No blackout no equipment failures no relay trips no regulatory violations, grid reliability compliant. Grid stability analytics platform reports system resilience margin declining for 11 consecutive months, no specific asset identified. Substation infrared no hotspots, DGA transformer healthy, breaker timing within limits, drone inspection no conductor damage. Renewable penetration increased 22 to 49 percent over 3 years. Battery storage installations increased 340 percent. Peak demand increased 11 percent. Near-miss relay interventions previous year 4 current year 29 no outages. Frequency corrections increased 3x over two years, automatic balancing systems working harder. Grid optimization AI deployed 14 months ago, automatic dispatch software upgraded 9 months ago, operators report system is becoming harder to predict. Inter-control-center communications latency increased 18 percent, several temporary communication failures. Senior grid stability engineer retired position remains vacant, average operator experience 12.4 to 6.1 years. Significant increase in automated control interactions, machine-to-machine commands, autonomous dispatch actions. Real-time power trading activity increased 280 percent, optimization algorithms thousands of dispatch decisions per hour. Grid analytics engine reports emergent network behavior increasing confidence 94 percent. Every discipline reports healthy, no group believes there is a problem.

cases.title: TEST 20 - ammonia terminal: vibration-induced fatigue governs over structural single-mode (settlement is the cause)
cases.transcript: Port of Santos ammonia export terminal, 16-inch cryogenic ammonia transfer line. UT lowest measured 0.394 minimum required 0.340 acceptable by code corrosion rate stable no leak. PT loading arm swivel two linear indications acceptable. Berth dolphins coating breakdown 30 percent localized corrosion 1-2 mm no structural concern. Subsea ROV one pipeline support slightly tilted 6 degrees no apparent damage. Operators report the line vibrates significantly during compressor startup, no vibration monitoring data exists. Design originally 3 startup cycles per week, new operating model 22 startup cycles per week, no fatigue reassessment performed. Startup cooldown reduced 12 hours to 4 hours no revised thermal stress analysis. Recent dredging seabed settlement near subsea support, the tilted support sits within the settlement area. Five years ago a nearly identical transfer line experienced fatigue cracking at the support location following increased startup frequency, vibration-induced fatigue not corrosion. Tropical storm within 72 hours wave height increase, loading on dolphins mooring subsea structures.

cases.title: TEST 21 - LNG tank: assurance failure governs; structural fabrication suppressed (settlement within limits)
cases.transcript: 160000 m3 full containment LNG tank, 9% nickel inner, prestressed concrete outer, 18 years. Floor UT average wall loss 1.8 percent maximum 3.1 percent below concern threshold. Acoustic emissions no active crack growth detected. Tank edge settlement 12 mm design limit 75 mm acceptable. Prestressing tendon inspection no broken tendons no corrosion detected. Leak detection no LNG leakage. Tank now cycles 15 fill drain cycles annually original design 3 cycles annually no reassessment performed. Original foundation report missing destroyed during corporate acquisition. Nearby container terminal reclamation millions of tons of fill added. Groundwater monitoring shows change nobody investigated. Settlement monitoring system failed out of service 18 months replacement delayed. Database migration lost 11 years of historical settlement records. Previous owner documented foundation sensitivity concern no details. Government geotechnical study regional subsidence 3 mm per year never reviewed by facility.

cases.title: TEST 22 - offshore wind turbine: software/control failure governs, fleet convergence over inspection (no physical defect)
cases.transcript: Floating offshore wind turbine WT-117, 7 years. Gearbox oil iron elevated 18 percent below alarm trend stable. Partial discharge minor within acceptance. Blade root UT no cracking no delamination. Monopile tilt 0.32 degrees design limit 1.50 acceptable. Splash zone coating degradation 18 percent within maintenance plan. Power prices record highs offline costs 420000 per day. Grid reserve margin critically low. Three sister turbines failed unexpectedly last year root cause never determined. All three failures occurred after software upgrades, WT-117 received the same upgrade. Vendor claims the upgrade unrelated no evidence provided. Anomalous network traffic observed 17 times in the past month investigation incomplete. Blade pitch actuator intermittently reports impossible positions fault self-clears maintenance dismissed as sensor issue. Major North Sea storm forecast 72 hours high cyclic loading. Common factors same software version same pitch controller revision same maintenance contractor no physical defect ever found.

cases.title: TEST 22 - ethylene cracker furnace must not be classified/dispositioned as an offshore platform
cases.transcript: Large coastal petrochemical complex. Ethylene cracker furnace. Determine if Furnace F-7 should remain in operation until the next scheduled turnaround in 18 months. Tubes. Mixed feed including heavier hydrocarbons. Coking rate increasing every year. Furnace draft sensors periodically drift. Tube metal temperatures remain normal. Senior furnace engineer retired 18 months ago. A similar furnace at another company suffered a catastrophic tube rupture from flow maldistribution from progressive coking; the rupture occurred in a tube that passed inspection 3 months earlier.
